error.signature: tf2.ExtrapolationException: Lookup would require extrapolation into the future

verdict.summary: tf2 transform lookup fails when requesting a transform at a timestamp newer than the latest available transform. Common when sensor data timestamp is slightly ahead of tf broadcaster, or when using sim_time with clock skew between nodes.

dead_ends.action: Use rospy.Time.now() / self.get_clock().now() as lookup time
dead_ends.why_fails: Current time may be ahead of the latest published transform. Transform publishers have latency. Requesting 'now' often extrapolates into the future.

dead_ends.action: Increase tf buffer size to store more transforms
dead_ends.why_fails: Buffer size stores history, not future. The issue is timing, not storage. More history doesn't help when the requested time is in the future.

workarounds.action: Use tf2_ros.Buffer.lookup_transform with timeout for blocking wait
Use Time(0) to get the latest available transform instead of a specific timestamp
workarounds.how: transform = tf_buffer.lookup_transform('map', 'base_link', rclpy.time.Time())  # latest available, no extrapolation

workarounds.action: When using sim_time, ensure all nodes use use_sim_time:=true consistently
workarounds.how: ros2 launch my_pkg my_launch.py use_sim_time:=true  # all nodes must agree on time source
